snippet:
I want to take a moment to highlight two incredible employees at the Walmart on 40 Hwy. First, Ms. Deb absolutely deserves her kudos. That woman is truly a customer service queen. During the busy holiday rush, another shopper and I were searching for poultry seasoning. The shelf was empty, but Deb did not stop there—she continued searching high and low until she finally found what we needed. I know she spent at least 10 minutes helping us, and she did it with patience and kindness the entire time. That kind of dedication is rare, and it made a huge difference.

I also want to recognize Jeremy, who was just as impressive. I asked him where to find an item, and instead of pointing, he personally walked me straight to it. Quick, attentive, and helpful—exactly what excellent service looks like.

Let me just say, the Walmart on 40 Hwy feels completely restored. It’s like walking into a brand new store, and the employees are a huge part of that experience. Thank you, Deb and Jeremy, for reminding customers what great service feels like!

snippet: Very very unhappy and disappointed. I understand there may be some delays when weather happens. I'm no stranger to this issue, so I am not upset with delays. However, when I can't contact ANYONE from the store that can help me I can't help but be angry with the lack of customer service or care the store provides. I have called multiple times (back to back) and it rings and rings until the ending operator speaks to end the call. I have called other departments where they either transfer the call (where it eventually ends since no one answers) or no one picks up at all and the call ends anyways. How does one get help when there is no one to reach out to? Why even have a phone number to call when you are just gonna ignore it? It's 2025, phones and other devices are a huge part of everyday life. Everyone knows this. So, Walmart, if you are wanting to use apps and such then have someone ready available to help on the other end of the phone. I now have a delayed same day order that was supposed to be delivered between 7 and 9 am. I've received 2 messages saying it was delayed for a certain amount of time. After the 2nd message with it saying 45 min delay, I haven't received any notifications. That message was at 10 am. It is now 4:30 pm. I can't change to pick up order because it won't let me. It hasnt let me change it since this morning and no one would answer the phone then either. So now I have a pending hold on my card, no groceries and no one to help me. Do better!

snippet: I’m giving a 1⭐️ because I can’t do 0.  I ordered parsnips in my groceries today for delivery and when I got them, they were so rotten. They were dripping with rot juice.  You might ask why don’t I just go get my own groceries. Well I am a full-time caregiver for my elderly parents and my mother has been in and out of the hospital for the last two Years and it’s difficult to leave in case she might need my help in an emergency. I placed an order with the store two weeks ago and watched the progress of the delivery until it was one minute away from my house and all of a sudden it switched to delivered. The picture that was sent to me by the delivery person Was pitch black and my groceries never arrived. I am still waiting for a refund. When I returned my rotten produce today, which was not just the parsnips, but also my eggplant and my zucchini. They had brought juice all over them from the Parsnips. The last time I was in the store to purchasing on my own, there was a young lady that was shopping in order for someone. I waited for her to step back and as she stepped back, I came on through the aisle. I happen to stop to pick something up really quick and she said to me “oh that’s nice. Just stop right in front of me“. I was so taken back. I didn’t really even know what to say to her so rather than be confrontational, I let it go. Do not ever order groceries to be delivered from this store, for they do not care and the employees are simply there for a paycheck (Of which some don’t even earn).

snippet: I’ve had mixed experiences at this particular Walmart super center. Employees usually don’t know where anything is and look annoyed/irritated when you ask them a simple question so you’re better off just walking around aimlessly until you find that $2 item you couldn’t find no where else. And for the love of God, accept ApplePay! It’s 2025 not 2002. Shelves always look like garbage so it’s obvious the employees don’t care (which I understand, short staffed and underpaid, typical corporate America) or management doesn’t care either because either way they get their lil bonus at the end of the month so it doesn’t matter. That clearance isle is hell on earth, good luck trying to find any deals. It’s just filled with expired products or junk nobody cares about or wants. I don’t understand how this store has millions of employees but only has one person working electronics? I shouldn’t have to flag down the only employee working electronics so I can get some $5 earbuds. If you’re going to lock up the entire store, might as well staff it, right? They always looked bothered when having to unlock something for customers.. I work retail too so I get it to an extent but come on now. Security there is a joke. I had my wallet stolen outside where the vending machines are and Security was useless. I know they’re really just hired to protect the store and what little they have on shelves but still. If you can afford a high dollar security company have them cover all aspects of the store.. not just what’s on sale inside. Also, you don’t need to show them your receipt at the door, they can ask for it politely, but they can’t force you to show it because it’s not a membership store like Sam’s Club or Cosco.

snippet:
On Sunday, I was waiting on an electric cart. Twice, they gave a cart to people who just walked in. I waited over 2 hours. I have Chronic Pain and movement issues. Shopping is one of the hardest things I do.
Today, I found all my things, went to self checkout. I used my ucard and i needed tp pay for the rest and my wallet was not in my purse. I asked the lady what do I do. She said, " I have to check (I could not understand what she said)
She held her phone up in the air and swing it to the left. I did not understand what she was doing. I thought she was going to customer service. She did the same thing twice more then she said, "you need to get out of the way" I asked if I should go get my wallet" She said  "Well, you can." Very hatefully. I said  "You don't have to be so mean. I didn't do it on purpose. Can I put my things in the cart and come back for them. I am just going to my car." She said "yes, whatever you feel like doing" very hatefully.
When I came back in I handed my card and they said you have to go all the way back to the end of the line" I said, I paid for part of it already with my UCard." Another lady said, " You didn't pay for anything." I turned to get my groceries and they were gone. I asked where are my groceries? The second lady said, "I dont know maybe customer service" I said, "I don't understand, she said I could leave them right here". She said  "I don't know what to tell you." I started to cry and went to customer service. I told them what happened and a bald man seemed concerned. He went to talk to them. When he came back, he said,"They said they waited and waited and you didn't come back." I said "I was only gone about fives." He said, "you didn't pay for anything they voided the whole ticket" I asked where are my groceries. They have a cart full of things people had left but mine was not there. He said, "I don't know. You didn't come back and they voided it" I said I was going to report this and write a review." He said  "Go for it. Do what you have to do." And dismissed me.

The amount of pain I go through while I am there is bad and exhaust me to the point I can't do anything else. I can't just start over.

In addition, while I am trying to solve this, clerks kept running up to me asking if they could have my cart. Seven different people ask me for my cart. I got angry finally and said   very firmly, " please stop asking me!"
The store has far too little carts available and to stand in front of my cart and to hold it with your hands so I can't keep going is not right. I was already very upset trying to get my groceries back. I don't mind them asking but I kept saying, "when I am done." Then they would throw up there hands. It was a nightmare!!!

snippet: Wish I could give negative stars. I have been using the Walmart delivery almost since it's become available. I would say about 60% of the time something is messed up. Last night I was supposed to get an order from between 6:00 and 8:00 p.m.. I kept checking to see where my order was and by 9:30. I finally started a chat with an unhelpful woman. I was told that my driver picked up my order (false in my opinion) but had a medical emergency and had to leave immediately. Apparently with all of my groceries. It seems to me this is the second time a driver has had a medical emergency. It's about the fifth time I didn't receive my order. When I looked at the online order status it still said in progress. It never said someone picked up my order. I think the store just forgot. The unhelpful lady on the chat offered to set me up to get my groceries the next morning and I picked a time slot that would work for me and then all of a sudden that time slot wasn't available. You would think that after I waited 3 and 1/2 hours and still didn't get my groceries that you would make an exception for me the next morning. You can never get through to the store. If for some miracle you do everything is your own fault. The thing is, they do not care one bit that not only do you not get your stuff but you've wasted a bunch of time. So I wasted 3 and 1/2 hours yesterday, then another half hour tying to get everything canceled and refunded. Now I'm up super early so I'm able to go to a different store and get the groceries I need before I get in with everything else I have scheduled today.  This happens far too often. An apology goes a long way, but you certainly won't be getting one from this Walmart. And I don't want your stinking $10 coupon. Terrible, terrible. Terrible service at this store. Always has been always will be. Unfortunately, it's the closest Walmart and the only Walmart that will deliver to me.

snippet: Ordered curbside pickup for groceries between 9-10 PM on 1/21/23.  Was not able to select that we were on the way at 9:50, nor select that we were there at 9:55.  App said we had no order available to pick up.  After calling the store phone and curbside phone number and getting no answer, I walked in.  Found a manager who called up a curbside associate/manager named Charles.  Charles proceeds to speak to me condescendingly and short the entire time about him being off the clock and having to help me with the groceries.  “I can’t hand them to you” and “you look confused haha” were some phrases he used.  He has me get a cart and follow him to the curbside doors between meat and dairy where he mentions multiple times that I couldn’t get it brought to the car because it was closed and tosses half of my stuff in the cart he had me get.  I’ve worked customer service before and have been asked to do things after my shift was supposed to be over; if I had the attitude he did I would’ve been written up or fired.  If I can’t get groceries brought outside at 9:55, why is there an option for 9-10 PM? If it’s a corporate issue or a problem with the app, why was that not mentioned, and how is it my fault or problem? The only store in the area I EVER have problems with for curbside is Walmart for some strange reason.  After this visit, I’ll probably never use their curbside service again.  I was polite and said thank you the whole time, but the way he talked to me, I shouldn’t have been so nice.

snippet: I would not give this store any stars if possible. The three times I have been in this store, I’ve had a bad experience, but the one I’m writing this review about takes the cake. I came home from work early around 4am and got to this Walmart around 4:50am. I was not aware this Walmart was closed during the night considering almost every Walmart is open 24 hours. I got there, and there were cars in the parking lot but not as many as usual. However, it’s 4am and extremely Icy and cold outside, so I figured that was the reason. When I got to the door, there were about 6 employees smoking directly in front of the entrance and they just greeted me as I walked in. Did not hear anything else. I just needed diapers for my baby so I was quick to get them and get to the check out, when an employee stopped me and told me they were closed. I couldn’t believe it. I told her there were a huge group of employees outside and they just let me in. She didn’t understand why they didn’t say anything. She apologized and made it clear I needed to leave. So I set the diapers down and left. As I left, the same employees were there and I heard one guy mutter the hours under his breath indirectly at me. I turned around and asked, in an unhostile tone, why didn’t they say anything and just let me walk in. The one guy who spoke earlier said they tried to tell me but I was “too fast.” And they all started laughing at me. I just said I obviously didn’t hear and then walked back to my truck. It was absolutely humiliating and shouldn’t have happened. I understand I should have known the hours but I hardly ever go to this Walmart and it’s right by my home so I needed to go there. One of those employees should have tried harder to stop me and not let me shop. And then laugh at me when I confronted them. Extremely poor customer service. Even if it’s after hours, they represent the company and should care a little more. I’m very upset and will never set foot there again except for possibly contacting a manager there today.
